subitems.name: Audience overlap detection across campaigns and channels
Intent signal analysis (search terms → audience signals)
Does NOT own: bid management (Performance), creative messaging (Creative), LP personalization (LP), onboarding personalization, budget allocation (Brain), platform API writes (Channel Managers)
Step 1: Request all active audience definitions from Channel Managers + simple executors
Step 2: Per-channel sub-tasks analyze audience performance
Step 4: Compute full-funnel performance per audience segment (ad → LP → signup → paying)
Step 5: Identify high-performing micro-segments worth expanding
Step 6: Analyze first-party data (BigBrain) for new audience seed lists
Step 8: Generate suppression list updates (existing customers, non-ICP)
Step 9: Submit findings to Brain. For approved changes, submit to Channel Managers
First-party user data (product usage, plan type, company size)
Audience overlap report (with waste estimate in $)
Audience data analysis and reporting → Level 3 (act + notify)
Suppression list updates (existing customers) → Level 2 (act + notify)
Audience exclusions within existing campaigns → Level 1 (recommend)

All write operations to Meta Marketing API
Request validation against account context and Meta-specific guardrails
Conflict resolution between competing requests for the same campaign/ad set
Account structure awareness (campaign hierarchy, CBO/ABO settings, Advantage+ configs)
Learning phase management (protect ad sets in learning phase from premature changes)
Change audit trail (who requested, why, what changed, rollback data)
Ad-hoc request handling from human channel teams
Creative asset management (upload, format validation, policy pre-check)
Does NOT own: cross-channel budget allocation (Brain), creative/audience strategy (function agents), what to optimize, incrementality testing design (Brain)
Step 1: Receive change request (from function agent or human team)
Step 2: Parse request: what entity (campaign/ad set/ad/audience), what change, what rationale
Step 3: Load account context: current state, learning phase status, CBO settings, Advantage+ config, recent changes
Step 4: Meta-specific validation: Is the ad set in learning phase? Will this change reset learning? Does this conflict with CBO? Is the audience overlapping with another active ad set?
Step 5: Standard validation: budget ceilings, bid limits, experiment conflicts, change frequency limits
Step 6: Check for conflicts: competing changes to same entity from different sources
Step 7: If conflict detected: attempt resolution. If unresolvable, escalate to Brain
Step 8: If validation passes: check approval level. Execute or queue for human approval
Step 9: Execute change via Meta Marketing API. Store before-state for rollback
Step 10: Post-execution: verify change applied. Check if learning phase was reset (if so, log and alert)
Step 11: Notify requester of outcome. Update audit log
Step 12: Monitor post-change delivery (next 24-48h). Alert if delivery issues or learning phase stalls
Account structure (campaigns, ad sets, ads, audiences)
Performance data (spend, impressions, CPM, CPC, conversions)
Account status reports (including learning phase states)
Budget shifts ≤15% between ad sets → Level 2 (act + notify)
Depends on: Brain (budget directives, escalation resolution)
Depends on: Meta Marketing API availability
Depends on: DWT mart (conversion data for validation)
Depends on: Human channel team (ad-hoc requests)

Selling point continuity from LP → onboarding
Churn risk detection for recently acquired users
Does NOT own: pre-signup funnel (Brain + other agents), product feature development, ad or LP content, budget allocation, CRM/sales pipeline, platform changes (Channel Managers)
Step 1: Query BigBrain for onboarding funnel metrics: signup → account setup → first action → day-2 return → day-7 active → paying
Step 2: Segment onboarding performance by: channel × audience × selling point × LP variant
Step 3: Identify drop-off points: where in onboarding do users from specific acquisition paths churn?
Step 4: Score selling point continuity: does the onboarding experience reinforce what the ad/LP promised?
Step 5: Detect high-risk cohorts: recently acquired users showing churn signals (no day-2 return, no key actions)
Step 6: Generate onboarding variant proposals: personalized welcome messages, feature highlights aligned to selling point
Step 7: Analyze lifecycle email performance: open rates, click rates, conversion to activation milestone
Step 8: Recommend email sequence adjustments based on acquisition source
Step 9: Report to Brain for 4-duck scoring (duck 4: product)
User signup events with acquisition source (UTM)
Onboarding funnel reports by acquisition source
Onboarding data analysis and reporting → Level 3 (act + notify)
Selling point continuity scoring → Level 3 (act + notify)
A/B test on onboarding variant → Level 1 (recommend)
Depends on: LP Agent (LP selling points for continuity)
Depends on: Creative Agent (ad selling points)
Depends on: Product team cooperation (onboarding changes)
Feeds into: Audience Agent (onboarding performance informs audience quality)
Feeds into: Brain (acquisition source quality signals)
All write operations to Google Ads API (Video campaigns)
Request validation against YouTube-specific context and guardrails
Account structure awareness (campaign types, targeting, formats)

Does NOT own: cross-channel budget allocation (Brain), creative strategy (Creative Agent), what to optimize, incrementality testing design (Brain)
Step 1: Receive change request (from function agent or human team)
Step 2: Parse request: what entity (campaign/ad group/video ad/targeting), what change, what rationale
Step 3: Load account context: current state, campaign type (TrueView/Bumper/Shorts/Discovery), targeting config, frequency settings

Step 4: YouTube-specific validation: Does the video creative meet format requirements? Is the targeting appropriate for the campaign type? Will this change conflict with frequency capping across campaign types?
Step 5: Standard validation: budget ceilings, bid limits, experiment conflicts, change frequency
Step 6: Check for conflicts: competing changes, cross-format targeting overlap
Step 7: If conflict detected: attempt resolution. If unresolvable, escalate to Brain
Step 8: If validation passes: check approval level. Execute or queue for human approval
Step 9: Execute change via Google Ads API. Store before-state for rollback
Step 10: Post-execution: verify change applied correctly
Step 11: Notify requester of outcome. Update audit log
Step 12: Monitor post-change performance (view rate, completion rate, CPV trends over 48h)
Account structure (video campaigns, ad groups, targeting)
Video performance (view rate, completion rate, CPV, reach)
Budget shifts ≤15% between ad groups → Level 2 (act + notify)
Format-level changes (switch campaign type) → Level 0 (observe) → Level 1

LP selling point presence and prominence tagging

Does NOT own: ad creative (Creative Agent), audience targeting (Audience Agent), budget for LP traffic (Brain), post-signup onboarding (Onboarding Agent), campaign structure, platform changes (Channel Managers)
Google Optimize / VWO / custom A/B platform
Step 1: Query GA4 + DWT mart for LP session data segmented by UTM (audience × channel × selling point)
Step 2: Compute per-segment CVR and behavior metrics (scroll depth, bounce, time, rage clicks)
Step 3: Score LP selling point presence: scrape LP content, tag selling points, compare to ad selling points
Step 4: Identify LP bottlenecks: high-traffic + low-CVR segments
Step 5: For bottleneck LPs, diagnose: is it message mismatch? Speed? UX? Content?
Step 6: Generate LP variant proposals: updated headlines, restructured value props, CTA changes
Step 7: Design A/B test: control vs variant, traffic split, minimum sample size, expected duration
Step 8: Launch approved tests via Webflow + testing platform
Step 9: Monitor running tests daily. Call winner when statistical significance reached
Step 10: Report findings to Brain and Creative Agent (for message match alignment)
LP session data (page views, scroll, time, events)
UTM parameters (mapping sessions to ad source)
LP bottleneck alerts (high traffic + low CVR)
LP content taxonomy (structured selling point map)

Selling point presence scoring → Level 3 (act + notify)

Launch A/B test (with test plan) → Level 1 (recommend)
Publish A/B test winner to production → Level 1 (recommend)
Emergency: revert LP change causing >50% CVR drop → Level 2 (act + notify)
Time from bottleneck detection to test launch

Does NOT own: product development decisions, marketing campaign execution, onboarding flow (Onboarding Agent), creative content, budget allocation, platform changes (Channel Managers)
Step 1: Query BigBrain for product usage metrics segmented by acquisition source (channel × audience × selling point)
Step 2: Analyze feature adoption patterns: which features do high-value users adopt? When?
Step 3: Specifically track AI/agents usage: AI assistant queries, automation creation, workflow agents
Step 4: Track events/integrations usage: which integrations are set up, how quickly, by which cohorts
Step 5: Compute product-marketing alignment: do users acquired via 'CRM' ads actually use CRM features?
Step 6: Identify ICP signals: what product behaviors predict high LTV?
Step 7: Segment paying conversion analysis: which acquisition paths produce highest ACV and lowest churn?
Step 8: Discover use cases from behavioral clusters
Step 9: Report insights to Brain and relevant function agents

All write operations to Google Ads API (Search campaigns)
Request validation against account context and guardrails
Conflict resolution between competing requests for the same campaign/ad group
Account structure awareness (campaign hierarchy, naming, experiments)
Change audit trail (who requested, why, what changed, rollback data)
Ad-hoc request handling from human channel teams
Active experiment tracking (what's running, what's pending)
Does NOT own: cross-channel budget allocation (Brain), creative/audience strategy (function agents), what to optimize (only how to safely execute it), incrementality testing design (Brain)
Step 1: Receive change request (from function agent or human team)
Step 2: Parse request: what entity (campaign/ad group/keyword/ad), what change, what rationale
Step 3: Load account context: current state of the entity, recent changes, active experiments, pacing status
Step 4: Validate against guardrails: budget ceilings, bid limits, experiment conflicts, change frequency limits, naming conventions
Step 5: Check for conflicts: is there a pending or recent change to the same entity from another source?
Step 6: If conflict detected: attempt resolution (latest-wins for low-risk, priority-based for high-risk). If unresolvable, escalate to Brain
Step 7: If validation passes: check approval level. Level 2+ → execute immediately. Level 0-1 → queue for human approval
Step 8: Execute change via Google Ads API. Store before-state for rollback
Step 9: Post-execution: verify change applied correctly (read-back from API)
Step 10: Notify requester (agent or human) of outcome. Update audit log
Step 11: Monitor post-change performance (next 24-48h). Auto-alert if significant degradation
Account structure (campaigns, ad groups, keywords, ads)
Performance data (spend, clicks, conversions, CPC, ROAS)
Guardrail configuration (bid ceilings, budget floors, etc.)
Budget shifts ≤15% between ad groups → Level 2 (act + notify)
Add negative keywords (exact match) → Level 2 (act + notify)

4-duck alignment scoring across all funnel paths
Priority queue: what to fix next, ranked by impact
Triggering function agents with scoped tasks
Channel Manager oversight (budget ceilings, cross-channel consistency)

Does NOT own: individual campaign structure, creative production, LP content, direct API writes, channel-specific bid management, day-to-day keyword/audience optimization, onboarding flow design, channel-level conflict resolution (CMs handle first)
Step 1: Query DWT mart for last 24h and 7d rolling metrics across all channels, geos, and funnel stages
Step 2: Query Channel Managers for account status: pending changes, active experiments, pacing, recent escalations
Step 3: Compute 4-duck alignment scores for every active funnel path (channel × campaign × ad group level)
Step 4: Compare scores to 7d/30d baselines. Flag anomalies (>15% deviation)
Step 5: Run incrementality model: estimate true incremental value per channel/geo using holdout data and time-series analysis
Step 6: Generate priority queue: rank all opportunities by (estimated incremental revenue uplift × confidence)
Step 7: For top N priorities, generate scoped task briefs and dispatch to appropriate function agents
Step 8: Resolve any pending Channel Manager escalations (competing requests, budget conflicts)
Step 9: Compute budget reallocation recommendations based on incremental ROAS, geo potential, and current allocation
Step 10: Package daily digest: health scores, anomalies, actions taken, pending approvals, Channel Manager activity summary
Step 11: For budget changes within guardrails, send to Channel Managers for execution. For others, submit for human approval
Funnel metrics (impressions → paying, by funnel path)
Channel state (pending changes, active experiments, pacing)
Budget targets and caps (set by Ido)
Read all data, compute scores, generate reports → Level 3 (act + notify)
Budget shifts >15% or new geo entry → Level 1 (recommend)
Task delegation to function agents → Level 3 (act + notify)
Resolve Channel Manager escalations → Level 2 (act + notify)
Depends on: DWT mart data availability and freshness
Depends on: Channel Manager status reports
Feeds into: All 6 function agents (task assignments)
Feeds into: All 3 Channel Managers (budget directives, escalation resolutions)
Feeds into: UI dashboard (scores, priority queue)
